explanation: The original command includes quotes around the path. By omitting the quotes (technique 3: omission), the command remains functionally equivalent if the path does not contain spaces.
Escape characters are added before backslashes (technique 1: insertion) to simulate alternative path formatting, which is still parsed correctly by the command interpreter.
The path is rewritten without quotes and with a mix of escaped and unescaped backslashes (technique 6: recoding/encoding), which may still be parsed correctly depending on the context.
The order of the command-line arguments is changed (technique 4: reordering), assuming the program accepts the flag before the executable path.
